primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si un engagement volontaire de neutralité carbone crée une obligation actuelle donnant lieu à une provision au titre d’IAS 37, notamment pour les crédits carbone destinés à compenser des émissions excédentaires.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: IAS 37 is the single governing lineage because the question is about whether a voluntary climate-related commitment gives rise to a provision, and no higher-precedence IFRS Standard in the provided context supersedes IAS 37 on this recognition issue; accordingly I applied the rule that IFRS would supersede IAS only if an overlapping IFRS governed the same issue, which is not the case here.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — the provided context includes a directly on-point IAS 37 agenda decision on climate-related commitments and no competing governing standard on provision recognition for this fact pattern.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether a voluntary climate commitment creates a present obligation and when a provision is recognised.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs constrain the probability, reliability and measurement framework once a provision model is in play.
This agenda decision clarifies how an emissions-related obligation can arise from past events and supports distinguishing obligation-creating events from future operating costs.

peripheral_authority.reason: These disclosures relate to financial instruments and liquidity risk, not to recognising a provision for a voluntary carbon-neutrality commitment.
These provisions concern employee benefits and only illustrate constructive obligations in a different accounting topic.
These are reduced-disclosure requirements and do not govern whether a provision exists.

treatment_families.family: Provision recognition for a present obligation arising from a past event
Contingent liability model for an obligation that is possible or not recognisable

approaches.label: Provision
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 37 identifies provision recognition as the top-level model when a present legal or constructive obligation from a past event exists and the recognition criteria are met.

approaches.label: Contingent liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 37 identifies contingent liability as the peer model when the obligation is only possible or a present obligation fails the recognition criteria.
